    Multipacting: a spline cavity¶

    Multipacting tracks electrons in a cavity’s monopole eigenmode field and counts secondary emission. It works for any geometry through cav.multipacting, and the setup is stepwise — each piece is set once and can be inspected before the sweep:

    step

    set

    inspect

    mesh

    set_mesh_parameters(h, p, pec_maxh)

    show_mesh()

    field

    compute_field()

    show_field('E'/'H', mode=i)

    launch sites

    set_emission_points(xrange, n_points, step)

    show_emission_points()

    		 
    import os, tempfile
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from cavsim2d import SplineCavity
from cavsim2d.utils.style import apply_style
apply_style()

EPKS = list(1e6 * np.linspace(1, 50, 20))     # peak fields to sweep [V/m]
PHIS = list(np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 36))    # launch phases

    The cavity¶

    A Bezier spline with a smooth iris: p1 and p4 sit at the beam-pipe radius, so the wall leaves the pipe tangentially (C1 continuity) instead of at a sharp corner.

    geom = {'p0': [0, 35], 'p1': [30, 35], 'p2': [42, 103],
        'p3': [78, 103], 'p4': [90, 35], 'p5': [120, 35]}
spline = SplineCavity({'geometry': dict(geom), 'beampipe': 'both'}, kind='Bezier')
spline.name = 'spline'
spline.set_workspace(os.path.join(tempfile.mkdtemp(), 'spline_mp'))
spline.plot('geometry')

    <Axes: xlabel='$z$ [mm]', ylabel='$r$ [mm]'>

    The field¶

    compute_field() solves multipacting’s own (wall-refined) field up front, so it can be inspected before the sweep — run() then reuses it. A finer wall mesh (pec_maxh) resolves the near-wall field the surviving electrons live in.

    spline.multipacting.set_mesh_parameters(h=6, p=2, pec_maxh=1)
spline.multipacting.compute_field()
print('modes [MHz]:', [round(f, 2) for f in spline.multipacting.field_frequencies])

    modes [MHz]: [1822.93, 3002.17, 3152.05, 3334.3, 3462.92]

    spline.multipacting.show_field('E')      # 'H' for the magnetic field; mode=i for HOMs

    BaseWebGuiScene

    Launch sites¶

    Emission sites are wall vertices, so a coarse mesh offers only a few in a narrow band. n_points interpolates extra sites along the wall — no mesh change.

    spline.multipacting.set_emission_points([0.04, 0.08], n_points=40)
spline.multipacting.show_emission_points()

    <Axes: xlabel='z [mm]', ylabel='r [mm]'>

    spline.multipacting.plot_sey()

    <Axes: xlabel='impact energy [eV]', ylabel='secondary yield $\\delta$'>

    The sweep¶

    spline.multipacting.run({'proc_count': 6, 'epks': EPKS, 'phis': PHIS})
mp = spline.multipacting
ax = mp.plot_counter()
ax.set_title(f'Spline cavity - peak c20/c0 = {mp.counter.max():.2f}')
plt.show()

    Multipacting Epk sweep: 20 points on 6 processes.

    Multipacting sweep done: 20 field levels, 1476 launch sites x phases, 185.9s (9.30s per field level).

    spline.multipacting.plot_enhanced_counter()

    <Axes: xlabel='$E_{\\mathrm{pk}}$ [MV/m]', ylabel='$e_{20}/c_0$'>
